title: glinus oppositifolius var. keenanii
intro: Glinus oppositifolius var. keenanii, also known as the Kenan's glinus or Kenan's prickly bladderwort, is a rare and endangered succulent plant native to the southwestern United States, specifically in the state of Arizona. This plant is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Glinus oppositifolius var. keenanii include its potential to treat wounds, skin conditions, and infections, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Herbal preparations of Glinus oppositifolius var. keenanii can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken orally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
